1: A direct: "His right hand and his holy arm have worked salvation for him." Luke 1:51 — "He has shown strength with his arm." Isaiah 53:1 — "to whom has the arm of the LORD been revealed?" (quoted in John 12:38 in connection with Christ). Acts 13:17 — Paul cites God's "great arm" in the Exodus, a pattern fulfilled in the resurrection. The "holy arm" that works salvation is the arm of the LORD at work in Christ.
2: A direct: "The LORD has made known his salvation; he has revealed his righteousness in the sight of the nations." Romans 1:17 — "in the gospel the righteousness of God is revealed from faith for faith." Romans 3:25-26 — the cross reveals God's righteousness publicly. The "salvation" made known to the nations is the gospel of Jesus Christ — the word yĕšûʿâ (salvation) is the Hebrew form of the name Jesus.
3: A shadow: "He has remembered his steadfast love and faithfulness to the house of Israel." Luke 1:54-55 — Magnificat: "He has helped his servant Israel, in remembrance of his mercy, as he spoke to our fathers, to Abraham and to his offspring forever." The covenant faithfulness God remembered is the faithfulness he enacted in sending the Son — the fulfillment of every promise made to Israel.
4: A revelation of God: "Make a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth; break forth into joyous song and sing praises!" Revelation 19:1-6 — "After this I heard what seemed to be the loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, crying out, 'Hallelujah! Salvation and glory and power belong to our God.'" The universal joyful noise that Ps 98 calls for is the hallelujah chorus of Revelation, celebrating the final salvation in Christ.
5: A revelation of God: "Sing praises to the LORD with the lyre, with the lyre and the sound of melody!" Revelation 5:8 — "the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb, each holding a harp and golden bowls full of incense." The lyre/harp worship of the Psalter is the harp worship before the Lamb in Revelation. The one before whom the harps play is the risen Christ.
6: A revelation of God: "With trumpets and the sound of the horn make a joyful noise before the King, the LORD!" Revelation 8:2 (seven trumpets of the angels); 1 Thessalonians 4:16 — "the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God." The trumpet noise before the King is the trumpet of Christ's return.
7: A shadow: "Let the sea roar and all that fills it; the world and those who dwell in it." Revelation 5:13 — "I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea, and all that is in them, saying, 'To him who sits on the throne and to the Lamb be blessing and honor and glory and might forever and ever!'" The sea roaring in praise of the LORD is the sea joining the cosmic worship of God and the Lamb.
8: A shadow: "Let the rivers clap their hands; let the hills sing for joy together." Romans 8:21-22 — "creation itself will be set free from its bondage to corruption and obtain the freedom of the glory of the children of God." The rivers clapping and hills singing represent creation's anticipation of the liberation that Christ brings — the new creation where the groaning ends and joy begins (Revelation 21:4-5).
9: A direct: "He comes to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with equity." Acts 17:31 — "he has fixed a day on which he will judge the world in righteousness by a man whom he has appointed." Revelation 19:11 — "in righteousness he judges and makes war." This verse and Psalms 96:13 form a pair — both the enthronement and new song traditions converge on the coming of Christ as universal judge in righteousness.
